title: Word Definitions, Tokenization, Boundaries, and Offsets · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved Bookbot run contains one plausible path, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, reporting, CLI, testing, performance, packaging, security, or release defect; identify the first failed boundary before changing code. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in tested word and token contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evide…
objectives: Define a word for the stakeholder, language set, punctuation, identifiers, numbers, contractions, and hyphenated forms before counting.
Compare whitespace, regular-expression, Unicode-boundary, dictionary, and language-tailored segmentation against labeled cases.
Specify leading, trailing, internal, curly-apostrophe, dash, and repeated-punctuation behavior with changed examples.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.text: A debug Bookbot team handles case 8640 during a digital-humanities corpus revision. Build deterministic Python evidence to tokenize under a named word policy with nonempty tokens and valid source offsets; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Enforce nonempty tokens, monotonic nonoverlapping spans, declared normalization, and total-count reconciliation.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Token Invariants
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-token-invariants-jleiev" that uses case 8966 to tokenize under a named word policy with nonempty tokens and valid source offsets. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Token Invariants.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Token Invariants into observable performance evidence. For Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Token Invariants, keep Token Invariants connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-token-invariants-jleiev
# Competency: bookbot-token-invariants.
# Case 8966: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion.
# Changed case: introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable.
def evidence_bookbot_token_invariants_jleiev(text_length: int = 42, token_count: int = 7, empty_tokens: int = 0, offsets_valid: bool = True, word_policy_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_jleiev = "jleiev-veielj-8966"
    assert evidence_variant_jleiev
    evidence_axes_jleiev = (
        "corpusjleiev",
        "policyveielj",
        "revision8966",
        "commandjleiev8966",
        "reportveielj8966",
        "test8966jleiev",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_jleiev)) == 6
    if text_length < 0 or token_count < 0:
        return False, token_count, "token-count"
    if empty_tokens > 0:
        return False, empty_tokens, "empty-token"
    if not offsets_valid:
        return False, token_count, "offset-contract"
    if not word_policy_named:
        return False, token_count, "undefined-word"
    assert token_count <= text_length
    return True, token_count, "bounded-tokens"
